---
title: "Practice Activity 1: What is Code?"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  error: true

author: SOLUTIONS
eval: true
---
## Code Tracing

Here is a piece of R code where we define variables and do some basic arithmetic.

```{r}
#| code-line-numbers: true
#| eval: false

x <- 3
y <- 4
x <- x + y
x <- x + y
z <- x * y
y <- 10
z <- x * y
```

5.  Fill out the Code Tracing Table for each line of code above.

| Line | `x`  | `y`             | `z`             |
|------|------|-----------------|-----------------|
| 1    | 3    | Does not exist. | Does not exist. |
| 2    | 3    |      4          | Does not exist. |
| 3    | 7    |      4          | Does not exist. |
| 4    | 11   |      4          | Does not exist. |
| 5    | 11   |      4          |        44       |
| 6    | 11   |      10         |        44       |
| 7    | 11   |      10         |        110      |
: Code Tracing Table: Enter the value of each variable at each line of the above code.

::: callout-tip
# Practice Activity Submission

What are the values of `x`, `y`, and `z` at the end of the above code chunk?

:::

x = 11
y = 10
z = 44
